question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts] Can an unprivileged external user acting as a reachable block node peer repeatedly send malformed SubscribeStreamResponse messages to exhaust maxSubscribeAttempts (default 3) on every configured block node, causing all nodes to be marked inactive and halting transaction ingestion entirely?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: maxBlockItems] Can an unprivileged external user submit enough transactions to a block that the resulting block exceeds maxBlockItems (800,000), causing BlockStreamException to be thrown in BlockAssembler.append(), triggering onError() and incrementing the error counter toward node deactivation, thereby preventing those transactions from being gossiped to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: readmitDelay] Can an unprivileged external user time repeated error injections to exhaust maxSubscribeAttempts on all block nodes just before readmitDelay (1 minute) expires, creating a perpetual deactivation cycle that permanently prevents transaction gossip from reaching the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: responseTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user acting as a block node peer delay serverStatus responses beyond responseTimeout (400ms), causing getBlockRange() to return EMPTY_BLOCK_RANGE, making the mirror node believe no blocks are available and skipping transaction ingestion for the duration of the attack?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: maxStreamResponseSize] Can an unprivileged external user acting as a block node peer send a SubscribeStreamResponse whose serialized size exceeds maxStreamResponseSize (36MB), causing the gRPC channel to reject the message and trigger onError(), incrementing the error counter toward node deactivation and preventing transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts] Can an unprivileged external user who controls a network path between the mirror node and all block nodes inject TCP RST packets to cause exactly maxSubscribeAttempts (3) consecutive gRPC connection failures per node, deactivating all nodes simultaneously and preventing any transaction from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: readmitDelay] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fixed readmitDelay (minimum 10 seconds, default 1 minute) window during which a deactivated block node cannot be readmitted to guarantee a sustained gap in transaction ingestion, effectively preventing gossip of all transactions submitted during that window?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: maxBlockItems] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sequence of BlockItemSet messages that individually stay below maxBlockItems but collectively accumulate pendingCount just above the limit across multiple onBlockItemSet() calls, causing a BlockStreamException that aborts the entire block and prevents all contained transactions from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: responseTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the minimum responseTimeout constraint (100ms) by introducing artificial latency on the block node status endpoint, causing repeated EMPTY_BLOCK_RANGE returns and preventing the mirror node from discovering available blocks containing user transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts] Can an unprivileged external user send a SubscribeStreamResponse with an unrecognized responseCase (default case in streamBlocks switch) to trigger BlockStreamException on every subscription attempt, exhausting maxSubscribeAttempts and deactivating the block node, thereby preventing transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: maxBlockItems] Can an unprivileged external user submit a flood of small transactions to a single block such that pendingCount in BlockAssembler exceeds maxBlockItems (800,000), causing the mirror node to throw BlockStreamException and discard the entire block, preventing all those transactions from being recorded?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: readmitDelay] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that readmitDelay has a minimum of only 10 seconds to perform a rapid re-deactivation attack: wait for readmit, immediately trigger maxSubscribeAttempts errors again, and repeat indefinitely to keep all block nodes inactive and prevent transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: maxStreamResponseSize] Can an unprivileged external user acting as a block node peer send a stream response padded to exactly maxStreamResponseSize (36MB) with garbage block items, causing the mirror node to accept the oversized response but fail during block assembly, triggering onError() and preventing legitimate transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that errors.set(0) only resets on successful response processing (not on successful stream end) to craft a sequence of partial successes followed by failures that keeps the error counter near maxSubscribeAttempts, causing intermittent node deactivation and transaction gossip gaps?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: responseTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 400ms default responseTimeout by introducing variable latency on the block node status API, causing the mirror node to intermittently receive EMPTY_BLOCK_RANGE and skip blocks containing specific targeted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: maxBlockItems] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial of transaction gossip by submitting transactions that, when combined with system-generated block items (block header, event headers, block proof), push the total pendingCount over maxBlockItems, causing the block to be rejected and all user transactions in it to be dropped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts] Can an unprivileged external user send a SubscribeStreamResponse.Code that is not SUCCESS (e.g., NOT_AVAILABLE, READ_STREAM_INVALID_START_BLOCK_NUMBER) to trigger BlockStreamException on each subscription attempt, exhausting maxSubscribeAttempts (3) and deactivating the node, preventing transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: readmitDelay] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the readmitDelay timer reset behavior in tryReadmit() to ensure that even after the delay expires, a single new error immediately re-deactivates the node before any transactions are processed, creating a permanent gossip blackout?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: maxStreamResponseSize] Can an unprivileged external user acting as a block node peer send a stream response that is exactly 1 byte over maxStreamResponseSize to trigger a gRPC RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED error, which is caught as a generic Exception in streamBlocks() and wrapped in BlockStreamException, incrementing the error counter toward node deactivation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxSubscribeAttempts has a minimum of only 1 to cause a single malformed block response to immediately deactivate a block node, and by targeting all configured nodes simultaneously, prevent all transaction gossip for the duration of readmitDelay?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: maxBlockItems] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Min(1000) lower bound on maxBlockItems to ensure that even with the minimum configuration, a block with 1001 items triggers the overflow check, and by crafting transactions that produce exactly 1001 block items, cause targeted block rejection and transaction gossip prevention?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: responseTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @DurationMin(millis=100) constraint on responseTimeout to ensure that even at minimum configuration, a 101ms delay on the status endpoint causes repeated timeouts, and by sustaining this delay, prevent the mirror node from ever discovering blocks with user transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts] Can an unprivileged external user send a BLOCK_HEADER block item followed immediately by another BLOCK_HEADER (without an intervening END_OF_BLOCK) to trigger the 'Received block items of a new block while the previous block is still pending' BlockStreamException in BlockAssembler.append(), exhausting maxSubscribeAttempts and deactivating the node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: maxSubscribeAttempts] Can an unprivileged external user send a non-BLOCK_HEADER item as the first item in a BlockItemSet when pending is empty, triggering the 'Incorrect first block item case' BlockStreamException in BlockAssembler.append(), and by repeating this maxSubscribeAttempts times, deactivate the block node and prevent transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: readmitDelay] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that readmitDelay is applied per-node (not globally) to deactivate nodes in a staggered fashion, ensuring that as each node's readmitDelay expires and it is readmitted, it is immediately re-deactivated, maintaining a rolling blackout that prevents continuous transaction gossip?
